summary: Accepted M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 after systems_architect created the M2 planning DAG, qa_reviewer requested dependency-order changes, systems_architect added the missing client dependency edges, qa_reviewer accepted Revision 1, and qa_reviewer separately accepted the final allowed_paths coordination-scope metadata. This acceptance covers planning artifacts only and does not accept downstream M2 implementation work.

tests_run.result: M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 is REVIEW before lead status update; 11 downstream M2 tasks remain DRAFT; prior tasks remain CLOSED.
No output while graph task remained in REVIEW.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON/DAG validation for all project/tasks/active/M2-*.json
tests_run.result: 12 M2 task files parsed; dependencies known; DAG acyclic; 11 downstream tasks remain DRAFT; client map/list tasks include both economy and route dependencies.

tests_run.command: node -e thread registry duplicate ID and route record check
tests_run.result: 35 registry entries with unique thread IDs; M2-TASK-GRAPH route records include required to_thread_id and send_input submission IDs.
Already up to date; completed using pnpm v11.0.0.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed.

decisions: M2 task graph is accepted as planning infrastructure only; all downstream M2 tasks remain unimplemented and require their own writer/reviewer gates.
M2-CLIENT-MAP-001 and M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001 intentionally depend on both economy/population and route/transport tasks because their acceptance criteria consume both read-model surfaces.
M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 allowed_paths intentionally include parent-owned coordination artifacts used for routing, handoffs, thread registry, goal-mode state, and model-routing state.
No product code, package code, content-source data, docs roadmap, accepted ADR, manifest, checksum, validation report, dependency, or implementation file is accepted by this task.
risks: M2 task graph acceptance creates DRAFT downstream tasks but does not prove M2 performance, host determinism, content correctness, or simulation behavior.
The next M2 task should be selected by the lead after integration; no downstream implementation task should be treated as READY merely because this graph is accepted.
requested_action: Commit, push, open PR, and integrate M2-TASK-GRAPH-001 if final gates pass; after main integration, close the task and then select the first M2 implementation/planning task without claiming downstream work is complete.
